prompt: Compare average monthly revenue between store regions for 2022 and 2023
data:
region,year,avg_monthly_revenue
Midwest,2022,10522.09
Midwest,2023,10821.44
Northeast,2022,17703.52
Northeast,2023,14970.11
South,2022,14925.39
South,2023,14578.71
West,2022,12241.59
West,2023,12129.5
answer: In 2022, the average monthly revenue for the Midwest region was $10,522.09, which increased to $10,821.44 in 2023. Conversely, the Northeast region saw a decrease from $17,703.52 in 2022 to $14,970.11 in 2023, while the South region's average monthly revenue dropped from $14,925.39 to $14,578.71, and the West region's revenue decreased from $12,241.59 to $12,129.50 during the same period.

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

group_col = config['group_by']
x_col = config['x_axis']
y_col = config['y_axis']

groups = sorted(data_df[group_col].unique())
x_labels = sorted(data_df[x_col].unique())
x = np.arange(len(x_labels))
n_series = len(groups)
bar_width = 0.8 / n_series

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
for i, group_val in enumerate(groups):
    df_group = data_df[data_df[group_col] == group_val].set_index(x_col)
    y_vals = [df_group.loc[xl, y_col] if xl in df_group.index else 0 for xl in x_labels]
    offset = (i - n_series / 2 + 0.5) * bar_width
    plt.bar(x + offset, y_vals, width=bar_width, label=str(group_val))

plt.xlabel(x_col)
plt.ylabel(y_col)
plt.title(config['title'])
plt.xticks(x, x_labels, rotation=45, ha='right')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
visualization_goal: Grouped bar chart comparing average monthly revenue per region between 2022 and 2023

chart_config.title: Avg Monthly Revenue by Region: 2022 vs 2023

sql_query:
SELECT st.region, s.yr AS year, ROUND(AVG(s.monthly_rev), 2) AS avg_monthly_revenue 
FROM (
    SELECT Store_Number, 
           EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS yr, 
           DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS month, 
           SUM(Total_Sale_Value) AS monthly_rev 
    FROM sales 
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) IN (2022, 2023) 
    GROUP BY Store_Number, yr, month
) s 
JOIN stores st ON s.Store_Number = st.Store_Number 
GROUP BY st.region, s.yr 
ORDER BY st.region, s.yr;
